heading: Step 2: Create the MongoDB Vector Search indexes.
body:
Run the following code in your notebook to create the
MongoDB collections and MongoDB Vector Search indexes used to store and query
your data for this tutorial. Replace `<connection-string>` with your
cluster's [connection string](manual/reference/connection-string).

> **Note:** 
>
> Replace `<connection-string>` with the connection string for your
> Atlas cluster or local Atlas deployment.
>
> <Tab tabid="cloud">
>   Atlas Cluster
>
>   Your connection string should use the following format:
>
>   ```
>   mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@<clusterName>.<hostname>.mongodb.net
>   ```
>
>   To learn more, see [Driver](connect-via-driver).
> </Tab>
>
> Local or Self-Managed
>
> Your connection string should use the
> following format:
>
> ```
> mongodb://localhost:<port-number>/?directConnection=true
> ```
>
> To learn more, see [Connection Strings](manual/reference/connection-string).

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

client = MongoClient("<connection-string>") # Replace with your connection string
db = client["AGENT-ENGINE"]
stars_wars_collection = db["sample_starwars_embeddings"]
stars_trek_collection = db["sample_startrek_embeddings"]

# Create your index model, then create the search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
   definition={
      "fields": [
         {
         "type": "vector",
         "path": "embedding",
         "numDimensions": 768,
         "similarity": "cosine"
         }
      ]
   },
   name="vector_index",
   type="vectorSearch"
)

# Create the indexes
stars_wars_collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
stars_trek_collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
```

To learn more about creating a MongoDB Vector Search index, see
[index definition](avs-types-vector-search).

codeBlocks.content:
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

client = MongoClient("<connection-string>") # Replace with your connection string
db = client["AGENT-ENGINE"]
stars_wars_collection = db["sample_starwars_embeddings"]
stars_trek_collection = db["sample_startrek_embeddings"]

# Create your index model, then create the search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
   definition={
      "fields": [
         {
         "type": "vector",
         "path": "embedding",
         "numDimensions": 768,
         "similarity": "cosine"
         }
      ]
   },
   name="vector_index",
   type="vectorSearch"
)

# Create the indexes
stars_wars_collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
stars_trek_collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
